confirmed_incidents.title: Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) — System Breach
confirmed_incidents.summary: On 17 March 2026 the MGA confirmed a breach of one of its internal systems. Lilith Wittmann of the Chaos Computer Club claimed responsibility via LinkedIn and X, warning any extradition attempt would trigger immediate release of her full iGaming data archive covering all 304 licensed operators. The MGA condemned the access but called the allegations unsubstantiated. Criminal investigation opened; Wittmann faces up to 10 years under Maltese law.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: MGA is Malta's primary gaming regulator; breach of its internal systems

confirmed_incidents.title: Fast Track (Malta CRM) — Third-Party Casino Data Breach
confirmed_incidents.records: PII + KYC + financials; majority of Shuffle Casino users
confirmed_incidents.summary: Malta-headquartered iGaming CRM provider Fast Track was breached in October 2025, exposing player KYC documents including passports and driver's licences. Shuffle Casino was the only publicly named victim. The breach occurred despite Fast Track holding a SOC 2 Type 2 certification renewed in June 2025.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Fast Track is headquartered in Malta and holds an MGA licence

confirmed_incidents.title: The Mill Adventure (Malta) — API Flaw Exposes 800K+ Players
confirmed_incidents.summary: Malta-based gaming provider The Mill Adventure had a critically vulnerable unauthenticated GraphQL API. Researcher Lilith Wittmann disclosed the flaw on 14 March 2025. Exposed: player IDs, names, IBANs, login records and 70,000+ identity documents. Germany's GGL issued a public reprimand against Malta-based subsidiaries.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: The Mill Adventure is Malta-based; GGL reprimand issued against Malta entities

confirmed_incidents.title: Saint James Hospital Group — Cyberattack
confirmed_incidents.summary: On 18 April 2025 Saint James Hospital Group confirmed a sophisticated cyberattack and immediately isolated all systems. A forensic investigation was launched into potential patient data exposure. IDPC and Malta Police formally notified — the first confirmed cyberattack on Maltese healthcare infrastructure.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Saint James Hospital operates in Sliema and Żejtun; patients are Maltese residents

confirmed_incidents.title: MTCA — Email Data Leak (~7,000 Companies)
confirmed_incidents.summary: The Malta Tax and Customs Administration accidentally emailed company names, tax IDs, registration numbers and contact details of ~7,000 Malta-registered businesses to unintended recipients. IDPC formally notified. Opposition demanded Finance Minister accountability.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: MTCA is a Maltese government authority; 7,000 Malta-registered companies' data exposed

confirmed_incidents.title: Melita (Malta ISP) — DDoS Extortion Attack
confirmed_incidents.records: No data breach — national internet disruption
confirmed_incidents.summary: On 13 January 2021 Melita — covering ~49% of Maltese broadband — suffered a sophisticated DDoS extortion attack. Internet dropped island-wide from ~12:30pm. Mitigated within hours. No systems breached, no data compromised. Reported to police.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Melita is Malta's largest ISP; ~49% of all Maltese broadband users affected

confirmed_incidents.title: Evos Oil Terminal Birzebbuga — BlackCat Ransomware
confirmed_incidents.summary: The Evos oil storage terminal in Birzebbuga, Malta suffered IT disruptions in February 2022 as part of a coordinated ransomware wave hitting 17 European oil and fuel terminals. Evos confirmed 'disruption of IT services' causing 'delays in execution.' All operations continued safely. The BlackCat/ALPHV group simultaneously hit Oiltanking and Mabanaft (Germany) and SEA-Invest (Belgium).
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Evos operates Malta's Birzebbuga oil storage terminal — part of Malta's critical energy infrastructure

confirmed_incidents.title: MSS — Phishing, Ransomware & DDoS Surge (Public Sector & SMEs)
confirmed_incidents.summary: MSS annual reports for 2023 and 2024, tabled in Parliament in 2025, confirmed a surge in cyberattacks coinciding with Malta's UN Security Council tenure and OSCE chairmanship. Incidents included phishing at public sector employees, ransomware on SMEs, and DDoS attacks. Mostly financially motivated; no direct link to diplomatic activity established.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: MSS is Malta's national security service; attacks targeted Maltese govt staff and Malta-based SMEs

confirmed_incidents.title: Transport Malta — Server & IP Systems Breach
confirmed_incidents.summary: Overnight 25–26 September 2020, Transport Malta's servers and intellectual property systems were compromised. Online services down for 5 days. Staff advised to monitor personal accounts. IDPC notified; magisterial inquiry opened. Investigation did not conclusively determine whether data was exfiltrated. Hack may have begun as early as November 2019.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Transport Malta is a Maltese government authority; IDPC and magisterial inquiry involved

confirmed_incidents.title: C-Planet IT Solutions — Maltese Voter Database Leak
confirmed_incidents.summary: In April 2020 a database on C-Planet servers exposed PII of 330,000+ Maltese voters including names, ID card numbers, dates of birth and political opinions. IDPC fined C-Planet €65,000 in January 2022 — at the time Malta's highest GDPR fine. Origin of the data remains disputed.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: 330,000 Maltese citizens' data exposed; IDPC Malta issued the fine; Maltese court proceedings
confirmed_incidents.source_name: noyb · The Shift News · Daphne Foundation

confirmed_incidents.title: Nationalist Party (PN) — Ransomware & Dark Web Data Dump
confirmed_incidents.summary: Cybercriminals hacked the Nationalist Party's servers, demanded €5,000 ransom and when PN refused to pay, dumped personal information of over 21,000 people on the dark web. PN believed the exposed data related to records from 2014 or earlier. Attack highlighted vulnerability of Maltese political organisations to ransomware.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Nationalist Party is a Maltese political party; 21,000 Maltese citizens' data dumped publicly

confirmed_incidents.title: FreeHour App — Vulnerability Disclosure & Legal Controversy
confirmed_incidents.summary: Four UoM students found critical API flaws in FreeHour (Malta's most popular student app). Instead of recognition, they were arrested, strip-searched, and had 14 devices impounded for 28 months. Two reported PTSD. The case triggered national debate, Budapest Convention adoption, and a Presidential Pardon in July 2025.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: FreeHour serves Maltese university students; students are Maltese nationals; case led to Maltese national law reform

confirmed_incidents.title: Bank of Valletta (BOV) — €13M Nation-State Cyber Heist
confirmed_incidents.summary: On 13 February 2019 the EmpireMonkey APT group transferred €13M from BOV via falsified international transactions to accounts in the US, UK, Czech Republic and Hong Kong. BOV shut down all operations within 30 minutes. €10.1M recovered; €2.9M located in Hong Kong. EmpireMonkey had previously targeted HSBC Malta in October 2018. Six UK arrests followed.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Bank of Valletta is Malta's largest bank; full national banking shutdown; Malta Security Services confirmed the attack

confirmed_incidents.title: Lands Authority — Google-Indexed Personal Data Breach
confirmed_incidents.summary: The Lands Authority's online portal was misconfigured, leaving 10GB+ of personal data including identity cards and passport copies of 5,000+ applicants publicly accessible and indexed by Google. Discovered by Times of Malta and The Shift News in November 2018. IDPC fined the Authority €5,000 in February 2019.
confirmed_incidents.malta_connection: Lands Authority is a Maltese government body; 5,000+ Maltese citizens' documents exposed; IDPC Malta issued the fine

court_cases.title: C-Planet IT Solutions — IDPC Fine €65,000 (Voter Data Breach)
court_cases.outcome: €65,000 fine — Malta's highest GDPR fine at the time
court_cases.summary: IDPC fined C-Planet €65,000 for unlawfully processing voter data of 330,000+ Maltese citizens. C-Planet appealed the decision. The origin of the voter database remains legally disputed with proceedings ongoing.

court_cases.title: Lands Authority — IDPC Fine €5,000 (Portal Data Exposure)
court_cases.outcome: €5,000 fine — first major GDPR fine in Malta
court_cases.summary: IDPC fined the Lands Authority €5,000 for misconfiguring its online portal, exposing 5,000+ citizens' identity documents publicly on Google. The portal was temporarily suspended. First significant GDPR enforcement action in Malta.

court_cases.title: FreeHour Ethical Hacking Case — Criminal Charges & Presidential Pardon
court_cases.summary: Four UoM students and their lecturer were criminally charged after responsibly disclosing API vulnerabilities in FreeHour. Devices impounded for 28 months. Case led to Budapest Convention adoption and Presidential Pardon in July 2025. Triggered national law reform on ethical hacking protections.

court_cases.title: MFSA vs E&S Consultancy — Court of Appeal: MFSA Liable for Data Leak
court_cases.outcome: MFSA found liable; ordered to pay costs
court_cases.summary: Malta's Court of Appeal confirmed the MFSA bore responsibility for leaking a confidential regulatory decision regarding E&S Consultancy Ltd to investigative website OffshoreAlert a full week before official notification. Forensic report established only the MFSA possessed the information at the time. MFSA ordered to shoulder costs.

court_cases.title: Multi-Bank Phishing Suspect — Arrested & Denied Bail
court_cases.outcome: Arrested February 2026; bail denied; trial pending
court_cases.summary: A 25-year-old suspect was arrested in February 2026 in connection with a phishing scheme targeting BOV, APS, HSBC Malta and BNF Bank, defrauding 100+ victims. International involvement suspected; cryptocurrency money laundering detected via Revolut and Bitcoin. Bail was denied by the court.

court_cases.title: €1M Fraud Case — Woman Arrested for Scamming 200 Victims
court_cases.summary: Malta Police and the FCID arrested a 25-year-old woman and an 82-year-old man in connection with a fraud investigation linked to losses of approximately €1 million affecting around 200 victims. Case referred to the criminal court for prosecution.

court_cases.title: BOV Cyber Heist — Six UK Arrests (EmpireMonkey APT)
court_cases.outcome: Six individuals arrested in the UK
court_cases.summary: Six individuals were arrested in the UK in connection with the EmpireMonkey APT group responsible for the BOV €13M heist. The case involved international cooperation between Malta, the UK, US, Czech Republic and Hong Kong. €10.1M was recovered.

live_news.title: NEW — Malta Police Warn of Surge in Spoofed Calls Impersonating Officers
live_news.summary: The Malta Police Force issued an urgent public warning about a surge in fraudulent phone calls where scammers impersonate police officers using spoofed local mobile numbers. Scammers increasingly use AI and deepfake videos — including one featuring PM Robert Abela — to target victims. Superintendent Jonathan Ransley warned 'everyone is a potential victim, not because people are ignorant, but because scams are getting more sophisticated.'
live_news.malta_connection: Malta Police Force issued the warning; Maltese citizens targeted; deepfake of PM Robert Abela used

live_news.title: MGA Breach: Wittmann Used LinkedIn and X — LinkedIn Post Rapidly Removed by Platform
live_news.summary: Lilith Wittmann posted on both LinkedIn and X claiming responsibility — the LinkedIn post spread rapidly across iGaming before being removed by the platform. She warned any police action would trigger immediate release of her full iGaming data archive. Three simultaneous processes are now underway: Malta's criminal investigation, Germany's awareness of Wittmann's statements, and Wittmann's possession of a data archive she is using as a bargaining chip.
live_news.malta_connection: MGA is Malta's primary gaming regulator; criminal investigation opened in Malta; data shared with Maltese authorities

live_news.title: MFSA Issues 'Heightened Cyber Threat' Circular to All Supervised Financial Entities
live_news.summary: The MFSA issued a circular titled 'Exercising Caution in Times of Heightened Cyber Threat', warning all MFSA-supervised entities of an elevated cyber risk environment. The circular follows 2026 Supervisory Priorities placing ICT and cyber-resilience under DORA as a top concern for Malta's entire financial sector.
live_news.malta_connection: MFSA is Malta's financial regulator; circular addressed to all Malta-licensed financial entities

live_news.title: MFSA Public-Private Anti-Fraud Partnership with Police, FIAU and Central Bank
live_news.summary: On 8 April 2026 the MFSA announced a formal Public-Private Partnership to combat financial fraud, bringing together the Malta Police Force, the Office of the Arbiter for Financial Services, the FIAU and the Central Bank of Malta. The initiative focuses on consumer-facing retail payment fraud with intelligence sharing across five pillars.
live_news.malta_connection: All five partner bodies are Maltese authorities; initiative targets fraud affecting Maltese consumers

live_news.title: BOV Fraud Losses Nearly Doubled — 'Spot the Scam' Tool Launched
live_news.summary: BOV revealed fraud losses nearly doubled from €2.9M in 2024 to €5.4M in the first eight months of 2025. BOV launched a free 'Spot the Scam Self-Assessment' tool in April 2026. 28.7% of Maltese enterprises reported a cyber incident in 2023 — above the EU average of 21.5% (Eurostat 2024).
live_news.malta_connection: BOV is Malta's largest bank; fraud losses involve Maltese customers; FCID is Malta's financial crime police unit

live_news.title: Multi-Bank Phishing: 100+ Victims Across BOV, APS, HSBC and BNF — Suspect Denied Bail
live_news.summary: A coordinated phishing operation using fake bank apps and SMS links defrauded 100+ victims across four Maltese banks. A 25-year-old suspect was arrested in February 2026 and denied bail, with investigators detecting international involvement and cryptocurrency money laundering.
live_news.malta_connection: Four Malta-licensed banks targeted; all victims are Maltese bank customers; suspect arrested by Malta Police

live_news.title: IDPC Enforcement Surge: CCTV Overreach and SAR Failures — 2024–2025
live_news.summary: Malta's IDPC significantly stepped up enforcement in 2024–2025. Recurring themes: failures to handle subject access requests under Articles 5 and 15 GDPR, and CCTV overreach capturing public spaces without justification. Financial penalties now imposed in more intrusive cases.
live_news.malta_connection: IDPC is Malta's data protection authority; decisions directly affect Malta-based data controllers

live_news.title: €1M Fraud — Malta Police Arrest Woman and 82-Year-Old Man
live_news.summary: Malta Police and the FCID arrested a 25-year-old woman and an 82-year-old man linked to fraud losses of approximately €1 million affecting around 200 victims. Case referred to criminal court for prosecution.
live_news.malta_connection: Suspects arrested by Malta Police; all victims are Maltese residents; FCID led the investigation

live_news.title: Saint James Hospital — Malta's First Confirmed Healthcare Cyberattack
live_news.summary: Saint James Hospital Group confirmed a sophisticated cyberattack on 18 April 2025, immediately isolating all systems. Forensic investigation launched into potential patient data exposure. IDPC and Malta Police formally notified — first confirmed attack on Maltese healthcare infrastructure.
live_news.malta_connection: Saint James Hospital is Malta-based; patients are Maltese residents; IDPC and Malta Police notified

live_news.title: Evos Oil Terminal Birzebbuga — BlackCat Ransomware Attack
live_news.summary: The Evos oil storage terminal in Birzebbuga suffered IT disruptions as part of a 17-terminal European ransomware wave attributed to the BlackCat/ALPHV group. Evos confirmed disruption causing delays in execution. Malta's critical energy infrastructure was directly impacted.
live_news.malta_connection: Evos operates Malta's Birzebbuga oil storage terminal — part of Malta's critical energy infrastructure
